name: Financial Management
description: Budgeting, Audits, Sanctions, Procurement, and Financial Control in Public Service.

subcategories.name: Budgeting & Financial Planning
subcategories.description: Appropriation, warrants, virement, MTEF, and planning cycles.

questions.question: The payment of emoluments and wages to public servants is primarily guided by which document, as referenced in the Public Service Rules (**PSR**)
questions.explanation: Financial Regulations contain guidance on the payment of emoluments and wages. Rule 040101 confirms that the FR governs the payment process.

questions.question: The legal instrument enacted by the National Assembly that authorizes the Federal Government to withdraw funds from the Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF) for annual expenditure is the:
questions.explanation: The Annual Appropriation Act, enacted by the National Assembly, is the legal instrument that authorizes the Federal Government to withdraw funds from the CRF for annual expenditure.

questions.question: How often must the Vote Book be updated to accurately reflect budget implementation and commitments, ensuring effective expenditure control
questions.explanation: Vote books should be updated daily to reflect accurate budget implementation and commitments Reference: Budgeting – Control Frequency.

questions.question: What is the technical term for the authorized transfer of funds from one specified budget subhead to another within the same expenditure vote
questions.explanation: Virement is the authorized transfer of funds from one sub-head to another within the same head of expenditure Reference: Budgeting – Virement Definition.

questions.question: Any request for the legal movement of funds between budget subheads (Virement) must be formally backed by:
questions.explanation: Virement must be formally approved and backed by a Virement Warrant Reference: Budgeting – Virement Authorization.

questions.question: Expenditure incurred by an **MDA** that exceeds the amount appropriated for that specific subhead without subsequent legislative sanction is termed:
questions.explanation: Spending that surpasses the approved budget is classified as an Excess Vote or unauthorized expenditure Reference: Budgeting – Irregularity.

questions.question: At the end of the fiscal year, all unspent budget balances must generally be:
options: Carried forward to the next year.
Returned to the Treasury (Consolidated Revenue Fund).
questions.explanation: At the close of the fiscal year, unspent budget balances are typically returned to the Treasury, specifically the CRF Reference: Budgeting – Year-End Closure.

questions.question: Which official document is used to request for the release of funds from the Treasury to the **MDAs** for execution of both capital and recurrent projects
questions.explanation: A Warrant provides for the release of funds for both capital and recurrent expenditures Reference: Budgeting – Funds Release.

questions.question: What is the consequence of incurring expenditure far exceeding appropriation that requires subsequent legislative action via a supplementary budget
options: A monetary fine on the Accounting Officer.
A formal query from the Auditor-General for unauthorized spending.
Immediate dismissal of the Vote Controller.
questions.explanation: Excess spending constitutes unauthorized expenditure and is reported by the Auditor-General, leading to legislative examination Reference: Budgeting – Unauthorized Spending.

questions.question: What must be checked in the **Vote Book** immediately before any commitment (like issuing an LPO) is made
options: The date the last Virement Warrant was issued.
The balance remaining in the relevant subhead.
The date of the last audit query.
questions.explanation: Before issuing any commitment, the Vote book balance must be checked to confirm that the expenditure is within approved limits Reference: Budgeting – Commitment Check.

questions.question: The **Medium-Term Expenditure Framework (MTEF)**, a key planning tool, requires government budgeting to focus on planning and projections over a rolling period of:
questions.explanation: The MTEF sets out government’s spending priorities and projections over a three-year period Reference: Budgeting – MTEF.

questions.question: Promotion arrears are payable within the year the promotion is effected and are calculated from which date?
options: Date of receiving the promotion letter.
Date the officer assumes duty in the new post.
Effective date of the promotion (1st January of the year).
questions.explanation: Rule 040104(iii) provides that promotion arrears are paid within the year the promotion is effected and are calculated from the effective date of the promotion, usually 1 January of that year.

questions.question: An officer, upon first appointment, shall be eligible for emoluments as from the date of:
questions.explanation: Rule 040102 confirms that on first appointment, emoluments shall, as a general rule, be paid as from the date of assumption of duty.

questions.question: Funding used to acquire or maintain fixed assets that benefit future fiscal periods is classified as:
questions.explanation: Capital Expenditure refers to funding used to acquire or maintain fixed assets that benefit future fiscal periods Reference: Budgeting – Classification.

questions.question: Which officer is primarily responsible for maintaining the departmental Vote Ledger (Vote Book), tracking budget allocations, and controlling expenditure commitments
questions.explanation: The Vote Controller maintains the Vote Book (also referred to as the departmental vote ledger) to monitor budgetary allocations and expenditures Reference: Budgeting – Control Officer.

questions.question: Who is responsible for preparing departmental financial statements at the end of the fiscal year
questions.explanation: The Head of Accounts prepares departmental financial statements at the end of the fiscal year Reference: Budgeting – Reporting.

questions.question: Which officer must sign all Payment Vouchers before payments are processed, confirming the correctness and classification of the transaction
questions.explanation: The Head of Accounts must sign all payment vouchers before payments are processed Reference: Budgeting – Payment Authorization.

questions.question: The primary responsibility of the Permanent Secretary/CEO as the Chief Accounting Officer is:
options: To manage the MDA's budget, expenditure, and compliance with Financial Regulations.
To approve all staff annual leave.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/CEO, as Accounting Officer, is responsible for financial management, expenditure, and adherence to Financial Regulations Reference: Budgeting – Accounting Officer Duty.

questions.question: What does the term **“Budget”** primarily represent
options: Detailed estimates of government revenue and expenditure for a fiscal year.
A legal document setting out procurement rules.
The annual report on financial performance.
questions.explanation: The Budget shows detailed estimates of government revenue and expenditure for a fiscal year Reference: Budgeting – Document Definition.

questions.question: An Officer's increment is typically withheld or deferred on grounds of unsatisfactory performance, or when the officer is under:
options: External training longer than six months.
questions.explanation: An Officer on an incremental grade level shall normally be granted an increment unless he is on interdiction/suspension or has a disciplinary action pending (Rule 040202).

questions.question: What is the standard incremental date for an Officer appointed or promoted to a post in the Federal Public Service
options: The date of appointment/promotion, under the applicable service framework.
The 1st January of the year following at least six months of service or promotion.
The end of the current quarter, in line with formal public service practice.
The 31st December of the current year.
questions.explanation: The incremental date of an Officer appointed or promoted to a post in the Federal Public Service shall be 1st January of the year following at least six months of service or promotion (Rule 040203).

questions.question: A staff on transfer shall be eligible for the emolument attached to his new office with effect from the date he:
options: Assumes duty in the new office.
questions.explanation: A staff on transfer shall be eligible for the emolument attached to his new office with effect from the date he assumes duty (Rule 040103) [24-26].

questions.question: If an Officer is promoted to a grade level that does NOT overlap his old grade level, he shall be placed at the:
options: Mid-point of the new grade level.
Minimum point of his new salary grade level.
Maximum point of the old grade level.
Next step above his previous salary.
questions.explanation: If the Officer is promoted to a grade level that does not overlap his old grade level, he shall be placed at the minimum point of his new salary grade level (Rule 040104(i)).

questions.question: The overall budget proposal for the Federal Government is prepared by which body
questions.explanation: The Budget Office of the Federation, under the Ministry of Finance, prepares the federal budget proposal Reference: Budgeting – Planning Authority.

questions.question: What document accompanies all government payments made in Ministries, detailing the expenditure, head, subhead, and required authorization
questions.explanation: All government payments must be backed by duly approved Payment Vouchers Reference: Budgeting – Payment Document.

questions.question: Who, as the Head of Department, must certify payments by confirming that services have been duly performed before payment is made to a contractor or service provider
questions.explanation: The Head of Department certifies that services have been duly performed before payments are made Reference: Budgeting – Payment Certification.

questions.question: What financial status occurs when government expenditure exceeds its revenue within a specific fiscal period
questions.explanation: A budget deficit occurs when expenditure exceeds revenue Reference: Budgeting – Definitions.

questions.question: What document is used to track all outstanding cash advances issued to staff and contractors against the budget
questions.explanation: The Advance Ledger is specifically maintained to record all outstanding advances to staff and contractors, ensuring recovery and control Reference: Budgeting – Advance Control.

questions.question: The total amount of funds allocated for staff salaries (Personnel Emoluments) in the budget is controlled primarily by data sourced from which platform
questions.explanation: The Integrated Personnel and Payroll Information System (IPPIS) holds the accurate data on staff nominal roll, grade levels, and steps, which determines personnel emoluments [34-36] Reference: Budgeting – Personnel Cost Control.

questions.question: Why are all financial commitments recorded in the Vote Book?
options: To ensure planned expenditure remains within the legal capacity of the approved budget.
questions.explanation: Recording commitments in the Vote Book helps ensure that spending stays within the approved budget and does not exceed lawful authority.

questions.question: Which **MDA** classification is authorized to manage its financial records internally but must still comply with **FR** and submit statutory returns to the OAGF
questions.explanation: Self-Accounting MDAs are authorized to manage their financial records internally but must still comply with FR and submit statutory returns.

questions.question: The fund established for the replacement of depreciating government assets is the:
questions.explanation: The Replacement Reserve Fund is used for replacing depreciating government assets [39-41] Reference: Budgeting – Reserve Fund.

questions.question: If an officer's increment is deferred due to unsatisfactory performance, the deferment must be fixed for a minimum period of:
questions.explanation: If an increment is deferred, the period must be fixed for not less than six months (Rule 040205).

questions.question: Decisions on deferment or withholding of increment and stoppage of salaries must be communicated to the officer concerned and simultaneously to:
options: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC), under the applicable service framework.
The Ministry of Justice, within established administrative procedures.
The Office of the Accountant General of the Federation.
The National Assembly, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Rule 040209 mandates communication of increment decisions to the officer and simultaneously to the OAGF, OAuGF, and OHCSF [43-45].

questions.question: If an officer's salary is withheld due to unauthorized absence from duty, the sanction is governed by:
questions.explanation: Rule 040206 allows salary to be withheld in cases of unauthorized absence (Loss of Pay).

questions.question: In budget terms, what is the specific classification of government spending on assets and infrastructure that create future benefits
questions.explanation: Capital expenditure refers to government spending on assets and infrastructure that create future benefits Reference: Budgeting – Capital Definition.

questions.question: The **Government Integrated Financial Management Information System (**GIFMIS**)** supports the budgeting process by providing:
options: A platform for political campaign financing.
Integrated financial operations, accounting, and expenditure tracking.
The exclusive mechanism for collecting tax revenue.
A register of physical assets only.
questions.explanation: GIFMIS supports real-time public finance tracking, integrating budgeting, accounting, and expenditure Reference: Budgeting – GIFMIS.

questions.question: If an officer is promoted across overlapping salary levels, the placement rule dictates the officer is placed at the:
options: Maximum point of the new grade.
Minimum point of the new grade.
Next salary point above previous earnings.
questions.explanation: If the emolument in the former post is higher than the minimum point of the new salary grade level, the officer will be placed on the next point, higher than their former emolument (Rule 040104(ii)).

questions.question: Which body prepares Nigeria’s annual budget performance reports for review
questions.explanation: The Budget Officer prepares annual budget performance reports for review Reference: Budgeting – Reporting Officer.

questions.question: The **Contingencies Fund** is reserved exclusively for which type of expenditure
questions.explanation: The Contingencies Fund is used to meet unforeseen and urgent government expenditures [53-55] Reference: Budgeting – Contingency Fund.

questions.question: Who authorizes payments from the Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF)
questions.explanation: Payments from the Consolidated Revenue Fund must be authorized by the National Assembly Reference: Budgeting – CRF Authority.

questions.question: The **Annual Financial Statement** is prepared to report on government's:
options: Financial performance within the fiscal year.
questions.explanation: The Annual Financial Statement reports on government’s financial performance within the fiscal year Reference: Budgeting – Reporting.

questions.question: If an officer is promoted and the new salary grade level does not overlap the old grade level, from what date does payment at the minimum point of the new grade level take effect?
options: The date of the FCSC approval letter.
The 1st of January of the promotion year.
The date of the promotion interview.
The date the officer assumes the new post.
questions.explanation: Where the new grade level does not overlap the old one, emolument payment at the minimum point of the new level takes effect from the effective date of promotion, typically 1 January of the promotion year.

questions.question: Within what time should promotion arrears be paid after promotion takes effect?
options: Payment within the year of effect.
questions.explanation: Promotion arrears should be settled within the year in which the promotion takes effect, not left indefinitely outstanding.

questions.question: Which budgetary authorization sets the maximum amount an MDA may legally spend within a fiscal year for a stated purpose?
questions.explanation: Appropriation is the legislative authorization that sets the maximum amount an MDA may spend within a fiscal year for a stated purpose. The item therefore tests the correct budgeting term for that legal spending limit.

questions.question: The Head of the **MDA** or Permanent Secretary must ensure that all personnel data entered into **IPPIS** are accurate and:
options: Verified by the Minister of Finance.
questions.explanation: Accurate information is essential for correct payment; the Head of the MDA certifies the records for IPPIS/payroll (Rule 040102).

questions.question: The authority responsible for granting a special increment to mitigate the lasting negative effects of an increment that was previously withheld rests with the:
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission is the body empowered to grant special increments (Rule 040207).

questions.question: An officer duly authorized to act in a post is eligible for acting allowance EXCEPT for any days in excess of how many days of continuous absence from duty on account of ill-health
questions.explanation: Acting allowance is paid except for any days in excess of 14 days of continuous absence from duty on account of ill-health (Rule 140130).

questions.question: Resettlement Allowance shall be paid at the rate of what percentage of an Officer’s annual total emolument
options: 1% of the officer’s annual total emolument.
2% of the officer’s annual total emolument.
5% of the officer’s annual total emolument.
10% of the officer’s annual total emolument.
questions.explanation: Resettlement Allowance shall be paid at the rate of 2% of an Officer’s annual total emolument (Rule 140136).

questions.question: Which practice best strengthens governance in budgeting and financial planning?
options: Approved budgeting procedure with complete records.
questions.explanation: Governance in budgeting and financial planning is strongest when officers follow the approved procedure and keep the full record needed for review.

questions.question: For effective budgeting & financial planning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure budgeting & financial planning compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk management in budgeting and financial planning?
questions.explanation: Risk management improves when material exceptions are identified early, escalated promptly, and tracked for follow-up.

questions.question: Which practice best supports vote-book control in budgeting and financial planning?
questions.explanation: Vote-book control is strongest when officers confirm budget availability before creating commitments and record the commitment properly.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain virement process in Budgeting & Financial Planning
options: Obtain proper authorization before moving funds between heads.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Obtain proper authorization before moving funds between heads. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Budgeting & Financial Planning, which action best demonstrates expenditure discipline
options: Commit and spend only within approved budget limits.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Commit and spend only within approved budget limits. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports audit readiness in budgeting and financial planning?
options: Complete supporting records for verification and audit.
Convenience ahead of audit trail requirements.
questions.explanation: Audit readiness depends on complete supporting records that can be reviewed, verified, and linked to each decision taken.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on cash management within Budgeting & Financial Planning
options: Reconcile accounts promptly and resolve variances with evidence.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Reconcile accounts promptly and resolve variances with evidence. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports documented procedure in budgeting and financial planning?
options: Complete records under the approved procedure.
questions.explanation: Documented procedure depends on following the approved process and keeping complete records of the steps taken.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Budgeting & Financial Planning
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which action best demonstrates public accountability in budgeting and financial planning?
questions.explanation: Public accountability depends on decisions that can be traced to recorded reasons and supporting evidence.

questions.question: For effective budgeting & financial planning, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best supports risk control in budgeting and financial planning?
questions.explanation: Risk control is stronger when identified risks are matched with documented mitigation and follow-up action.

questions.question: When handling budgeting & financial planning, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which practice best sustains operational discipline in budgeting and financial planning?
questions.explanation: Operational discipline depends on completing approved workflow checks before a matter is closed or advanced.

questions.question: In the context of Budgeting & Financial Planning, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Budgeting & Financial Planning
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which action best demonstrates governance discipline in budgeting and financial planning?
options: Approved budgeting procedure with complete records.
questions.explanation: Governance in budgeting and financial planning is strongest when officers follow the approved procedure and keep the full record needed for review.

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain budgeting & financial planning compliance in Budgeting & Financial Planning
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: In the context of Budgeting & Financial Planning, which action best demonstrates budgeting & financial planning risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which routine best sustains vote-book control in budgeting and financial planning?
questions.explanation: A sustainable vote-book routine begins with confirming available provision before any commitment is raised.

questions.question: Which practice best supports the virement process in budgeting and financial planning?
options: Proper authorization before funds move between heads.
Transfers outside the approved vote structure.
questions.explanation: The virement process is lawful only when reallocation is authorized and kept within the approved vote structure.

questions.question: When handling budgeting & financial planning, which choice reflects proper expenditure discipline standards
options: Commit and spend only within approved budget limits.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Commit and spend only within approved budget limits. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in budgeting & financial planning Reference: Budgeting & Financial Planning - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which routine best sustains audit readiness in budgeting and financial planning?
options: Complete supporting records for verification and audit.
Convenience ahead of audit trail requirements.
questions.explanation: Audit readiness depends on complete supporting records that can be reviewed, verified, and linked to each decision taken.

questions.question: When a compliance reviewer handles budgeting and financial planning, what should be done first?
options: Validate authorization, records, and control checkpoints before action.
questions.explanation: Financial control is stronger when authorization, records, and control checkpoints are validated before action is taken.

questions.question: What happens if a capital-expenditure amount conveyed by warrant has not been cash-backed to the CCA by year-end?
options: Automatic re-appropriation by the National Assembly.
Automatic carry-forward to the next year.
questions.explanation: A capital amount conveyed by warrant but not cash-backed to the CCA by year-end lapses automatically unless fresh authority is obtained.

questions.question: For capital expenditure, what happens to an amount conveyed by warrant that is not cash-backed to the CCA by year-end?
options: Automatic carry-forward to the next year.
Fresh re-appropriation by the National Assembly.
questions.explanation: For capital expenditure, an amount conveyed by warrant but not cash-backed to the CCA by year-end does not remain alive; it lapses automatically.

questions.question: What is the main role of the Public Accounts Committees of the National Assembly?
options: To manage and invest public funds.
To approve all new government contracts.
To scrutinize government expenditures based on the reports of the Auditor-General for the Federation.
To prepare the annual budget for Ministries, Departments, and Agencies (MDAs).
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To scrutinize government expenditures based on the reports of the Auditor-General for the Federation). The Public Accounts Committees scrutinize the reports of the Auditor-General for the Federation on the accounts of MDAs and call the accounting officers to explain any discrepancies.

questions.question: When may the Accountant-General issue funds for capital projects under a Development Fund General Warrant?
options: Upon the Minister of Finance's discretion.
After the Capital Expenditure Estimates have received approval by resolution of the National Assembly.
Only for projects exceeding a certain threshold.
At the beginning of the financial year.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (After the Capital Expenditure Estimates have received approval by resolution of the National Assembly.). Financial Regulation 320 states: "Such authority will be conveyed after the Capital Expenditure Estimates have received approval by resolution of the National Assembly."

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the chief aim of a Development Fund Virement Warrant?
options: To empower expenditure from the Contingencies Fund
To transfer funds from the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
To permit the issue of additional funds for the completion of a capital project where earlier funds were insufficient.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To permit the issue of additional funds for the completion of a capital project where earlier funds were insufficient.). Financial Regulation 324 states: "The aim of a Development Fund Virement Warrant is to permit the issue of additional funds necessitate for the completion of a capital project, for which the funds earlier provided... was insufficient or inadequate..."

questions.question: Who issues a Development Fund Virement Warrant?
options: The Minister of Finance after approval by the National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Minister of Finance after approval by the National Assembly.). Financial Regulation 324 states: "A Development Fund Virement Warrant must be issued by the Minister of Finance after approval by the National Assembly."

questions.question: What conveys authority to incur expenditure from public funds other than the Consolidated Revenue Fund and Development Fund?
options: A general directive from the President.
The appropriate warrant signed by the Minister of Finance, addressed to the Accountant-General.
A resolution from the Federal Executive Council.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The appropriate warrant signed by the Minister of Finance, addressed to the Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 326 states: "The authority to incur expenditure from any of the other Public Funds of the Government is the appropriate warrant signed by the Minister of Finance, addressed to the Accountant-General..."

questions.question: Besides payment vouchers, what other vouchers must the Internal Auditor audit and certify?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (All disbursement and revenue vouchers.). Financial Regulation 1703(d) states that the Internal Auditor's duties include auditing and certifying "all disbursement and revenue vouchers." Part 2: Financial Regulation: Board of Survey: Cash and Stamps

questions.question: Who is described as an Officer Controlling Expenditure?
options: The officer to whom the control of a subhead or specified amount has been delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer.
Any officer who signs disbursement vouchers. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The officer to whom the control of a subhead or specified amount has been delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 121 defines an "Officer Controlling Expenditure" as "the officer to whom the control of a sub-head, or specified amount of a sub-head, has been delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer."

questions.question: Who may exclude an item of expenditure from the Annual General Warrant when special control is desired?
questions.explanation: The Minister of Finance may exclude an item of expenditure from the Annual General Warrant when special control is desired, which is why that office is the correct answer.

questions.question: To whom may control of a subhead or specified amount be delegated under the definition of an Officer Controlling Expenditure?
options: Any officer who signs disbursement vouchers.
The officer to whom the control of a sub-head or specified amount has been delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer to whom the control of a sub-head or specified amount has been delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer.). Financial Regulation 121 defines an "Officer Controlling Expenditure" as "the officer to whom the control of a sub-head, or specified amount of a sub head, has been delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer."

questions.question: which option correctly addresses: Warrants issued by the Minister of Finance approve the Accountant-General of the Federation to disburse funds from which accounts?
options: Consolidated Revenue Fund and Development Fund.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Consolidated Revenue Fund and Development Fund.). Financial Regulation 103 mentions "Warrants issued by the Minister from time to time authorizing the Accountant-General of the Federation to disburse funds from the Consolidated Revenue Fund and Development Fund."

questions.question: Which warrant authorizes the Accountant-General to transfer sums appropriated from the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the Contingency Fund for replenishment?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 308 states that a Supplementary General Warrant is the authority for the Accountant-General to transfer sums appropriated from the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the Contingency Fund by way of replenishment. The item therefore tests recognition of that warrant.

questions.question: When is a Virement Warrant issued?
options: Need for a new project only.
Need for extra provision matched by savings under another sub-head of the same Head.
questions.explanation: A Virement Warrant is issued when additional provision is needed under one sub-head and matching savings are available under another sub-head of the same Head.

questions.question: Is virement from a Recurrent Expenditure Head of Account to a Capital Expenditure Head of Account allowed?
options: Allowed with Minister of Finance approval.
Allowed only in the last quarter of the financial year
questions.explanation: No. Financial Regulation 316(iii) states that virement from a Recurrent Expenditure Head of Account to a Capital Expenditure Head of Account, and vice versa, is not allowed.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Supplementary (Statutory) Expenditure Warrant?
options: To permit additional expenditure for votes chargeable against the Consolidated Revenue Fund by virtue of the Constitution.
To provide funds for new capital projects.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To permit additional expenditure for votes chargeable against the Consolidated Revenue Fund by virtue of the Constitution.). Financial Regulation 312 states it is for "additional expenditure over and above that included in the General Warrant or in a Supplementary General Warrant, in respect of votes which are chargeable against the Consolidated Revenue Fund by virtue of the Constitution..." 174

questions.question: From which fund is capital expenditure paid?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Development Fund.). Financial Regulation 318 states: "Capital Expenditure is paid from the Development Fund..."

questions.question: Which officer bears personal responsibility for obtaining acquittal for payments made out of public funds until the relevant payment vouchers are produced?
questions.explanation: The Sub-Accounting Officer bears personal responsibility for obtaining acquittal for payments made from public funds until the relevant disbursement vouchers are produced, as required by the Financial Regulations.

questions.question: Which classification code applies to both the Treasury Receipt Voucher and the disbursement voucher for pension deductions?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Cash transfer code 2000 series.). Financial Regulation 1907 specifies that both the Treasury Receipt Voucher and payment Voucher should be classified to cash transfer code 2000 series.

questions.question: Which financial report shows the income and expenditure of a government body for a specific period?
questions.explanation: The Statement of Financial Performance shows the income and expenditure of a government body for a given period. The item therefore tests recognition of the report that summarizes financial performance over time.

questions.question: According to Financial Regulation 103, whose signature must appear on a warrant before payment can be made?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 103 requires payment authority to come through a warrant duly issued and signed by the Minister of Finance.

questions.question: Which instruments are used to limit and arrange the disbursement of Federal Government funds?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 301 states that the Annual Estimates and Appropriation Act are the instruments used to limit and arrange the disbursement of Federal Government funds. The item therefore tests identification of those controlling instruments.

questions.question: Who is accountable for expenditure incurred without proper authority under Financial Regulation 301?
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The officer controlling the vote.). Financial Regulation 301 clearly states that "Any officer controls a vote, or part thereof, who incurs expenditure without such authority does so on his sole accountability and will consequently be held peculiarly in charge for his actions."

questions.question: At what point is a Provisional General Warrant issued?
options: At the end of the financial year.
When the Appropriation Act has not come into operation at the commencement of the financial year.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (When the Appropriation Act has not come into operation at the commencement of the financial year.). Financial Regulation 303 states: "A provisional General Warrant must be issued where the Appropriation Act has not come into operation at the commencement of the 'financial year."

questions.question: Can an unspent balance on a recurrent expenditure vote be revoted for the next financial year?
options: Yes, if approved by the Minister of Finance.
Yes, if approved by the National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 314 states that an unspent balance on a recurrent expenditure vote cannot be revoted for the next financial year.

questions.question: Which financial platform controls the total amount of funds allocated for staff salaries (Personnel Emoluments) in the budget
questions.explanation: IPPIS manages the personnel and payroll data crucial for controlling personnel emoluments.

questions.question: The process by which funds are legally moved between budget subheads within the same expenditure head is known as:
questions.explanation: Virement is the authorized transfer of funds from one sub-head to another within the same head of expenditure.

questions.question: What limit applies to funds brought forward by a Development Fund Supplementary Warrant?
options: Amounts above the total project cost.
Amounts below fifty percent of project cost only.
Amounts not exceeding the estimated total project cost in the approved estimates.
questions.explanation: Funds brought forward under a Development Fund Supplementary Warrant must stay within the estimated total project cost shown in the annual or supplementary estimates.

questions.question: How should expenditure be classified under the Financial Regulations?
options: At the discretion of the Accounting Officer.
Strictly in accordance with the Estimates.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 417 requires expenditure to be classified strictly in accordance with the Estimates.

questions.question: What financial responsibility is expected of the Board of a parastatal?
options: Managing the parastatal's bank accounts directly.
Preparing the parastatal's budget for submission.
Approving only the honorarium of board members.
questions.explanation: The Board of a parastatal is expected to prepare the parastatal's budget for submission through the supervising ministry.

questions.question: What policy objective is served by limiting funds brought forward by a Development Fund Supplementary Warrant?
options: Amounts above the estimated total project cost.
A ceiling tied to the estimated total project cost in the approved estimates.
questions.explanation: The policy objective is cost discipline: the brought-forward amount must remain within the estimated total project cost already approved in the annual or supplementary estimates.

subcategories.name: Procurement Under Financial Controls
subcategories.description: Procurement processes where FR controls and expenditure rules apply.

questions.question: The foundational legal framework governing the acquisition of goods, works, and services by all Federal **MDAs** is the:
questions.explanation: The Public Procurement Act (PPA) 2007 provides the primary legal basis and framework for managing public procurement processes in Nigeria.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Public Procurement Act, 2007
options: To ensure transparency, fairness, accountability, and value for money.
To fund the Ministry’s overhead costs.
questions.explanation: The PPA aims to ensure transparency, accountability, and value for money in public procurement.

questions.question: Which procurement method is the default procedure for public procurement under the PPA?
questions.explanation: The Public Procurement Act makes open competitive bidding the default method because it is the standard approach for transparent and competitive procurement.

questions.question: The procurement plan for an **MDA** must be based on and aligned with:
options: Historical spending from five years ago.
questions.explanation: Procurement planning must be aligned with appropriated budget provisions to ensure fiscal responsibility and is required under Section 14.

questions.question: What is the mandatory period for the archive retention of procurement records and documents
questions.explanation: Procurement documents must be retained for at least 5 years to support potential audits and inquiries (Section 34).

questions.question: The mandatory pre-qualification requirement for all bidders includes the submission of a valid:
options: Letter of Recommendation from the Minister.
questions.explanation: One of the essential prequalification requirements is the submission of a Tax Clearance Certificate (TCC), usually covering the preceding three years (Section 16).

questions.question: Which document is mandatory for processing payment to a contractor after goods or services have been received and verified
questions.explanation: Payment is not processed without the Job Completion Certificate, which confirms that work was done or goods were received Reference: Procurement – Payment Validation.

questions.question: Which of the following describes a **Responsive Bid**'
options: The cheapest bid received, under the applicable service framework.
A bid that meets all the technical and administrative requirements of the tender document.
A bid submitted by a government official.
The bid with the longest duration, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: A responsive bid is one that complies fully with all criteria stated in the bid document and is eligible for evaluation Reference: Procurement – Bidding.

questions.question: What is the primary function of **Bid Security** provided by bidders
options: To serve as a performance bond for the awarded contract.
To ensure commitment and prevent premature withdrawal of the bid.
To cover the cost of bid evaluation.
questions.explanation: Bid security is a financial guarantee that ensures the bidder’s commitment and discourages frivolous or unserious bidding (Section 28).

questions.question: The principle of **Due Process** in public procurement primarily emphasizes:
options: Rapid contract award regardless of procedure.
Adherence to legal and procedural requirements in awarding contracts.
Maximizing the margin of profit for contractors.
questions.explanation: Due Process mandates strict adherence to legal and procedural requirements to guarantee transparency and legality in government contracts Reference: Procurement – Due Process.

questions.question: The Accounting Officer (Permanent Secretary/CEO) chairs which committee responsible for ensuring procurement alignment with the budget and needs
questions.explanation: The Accounting Officer chairs the Procurement Planning Committee (PPC) established in every MDA (Section 21).

questions.question: Who is the officer responsible for ensuring compliance with procurement thresholds and the Public Procurement Act
questions.explanation: The Procurement Officer ensures compliance with procurement thresholds in line with the Public Procurement Act.

questions.question: The Public Procurement Act prohibits the practice of illegally breaking down a contract into smaller units to circumvent approval limits. This practice is known as:
questions.explanation: Contract Splitting is explicitly prohibited (Section 16 and Section 58(4)) as it undermines transparency and circumvents established thresholds.

questions.question: An officer involved in procurement fraud, such as collusion or over-invoicing, faces which penalty
questions.explanation: Officers involved in procurement abuse and fraud face dismissal or criminal charges (Section 58), as this constitutes serious misconduct.

questions.question: Which authority approves emergency procurements, subject to later audit and ratification
options: The Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Minister authorizes emergency procurements, often in line with procurement laws and thresholds, subject to later review Reference: Procurement – Emergency.

questions.question: The **PPA** mandates that the opening of bids for all competitive tenders must be conducted:
options: At the end of the contract period.
questions.explanation: Bid opening must be conducted publicly to ensure transparency and fairness (Section 27).

questions.question: The Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP) is established under which section of the **PPA** 2007
questions.explanation: Section 3 of the PPA 2007 establishes the Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP).

questions.question: The minimum time frame required for submission of bids under national competitive bidding is:
questions.explanation: Section 25 requires at least 4 weeks for submission of bids under national competitive bidding.

questions.question: The essential administrative procedure principle that ensures capability and eligibility is assessed before the formal submission of competitive bids is:
questions.explanation: Section 22 provides for pre-qualification of bidders to ensure capability and eligibility before submission.

questions.question: Which section mandates that evaluation of bids must be based strictly on criteria stated in the solicitation documents
questions.explanation: Section 33 mandates that evaluation of bids must be based strictly on criteria stated in solicitation documents.

questions.question: Which section allows for the cancellation of a procurement proceeding at any time before the contract award
questions.explanation: Section 31 provides that procurement proceedings may be cancelled at any time before contract award if necessary.

questions.question: All procurements above certain thresholds must be advertised publicly, as stipulated by:
questions.explanation: Section 23 requires advertisement of all procurements above the set thresholds in national newspapers.

questions.question: Which section requires the publication of contract awards above certain thresholds to ensure transparency
questions.explanation: Section 32 requires publication of contract awards above thresholds to ensure transparency and public access to records.

questions.question: Which section promotes efficiency and transparency in procurement by providing for the use of e-procurement systems
questions.explanation: Section 38 provides for the use of e-procurement systems in public procurement.

questions.question: **Section 57** of the **PPA** strictly prohibits which practice among procurement officials
questions.explanation: Section 57 prohibits conflict of interest and mandates disclosure of potential conflicts by officials.

questions.question: Which section prescribes imprisonment and fines for procurement-related fraud, misrepresentation, and bid-rigging
questions.explanation: Section 58 prescribes penalties for fraud, misrepresentation, and bid-rigging, including imprisonment [108-110].

questions.question: The BPP is empowered to suspend procurement proceedings if irregularities are detected, as stipulated in:
questions.explanation: Section 53 empowers the BPP to suspend procurement if irregularities are detected.

questions.question: **Section 16** of the **PPA** requires eligible contractors to demonstrate that they are currently not:
questions.explanation: Section 16 states that eligible contractors must not be in receivership, bankruptcy, or insolvency.

questions.question: Procurement of goods and works is prohibited without:
options: Approved budgetary provision and availability of funds.
questions.explanation: Section 14 prohibits procurement without approved budgetary provision and availability of funds.

questions.question: The primary objective of pre-qualification of bidders in the public procurement process is:
options: To ensure capability and eligibility before submission of bids.
To reduce the number of potential contractors drastically.
To bypass the Ministerial Tenders Board.
questions.explanation: Pre-qualification is intended to determine the bidders' technical and professional capacity before receiving main tenders Reference: PPA – Pre-qualification objective.

questions.question: Which section empowers the BPP to inspect procurement documents and records of entities
questions.explanation: Section 5 empowers the BPP to inspect procurement records to ensure compliance.

questions.question: Under which section of the PPA is the BPP empowered to conduct procurement audits?
questions.explanation: Section 6 of the Public Procurement Act empowers the Bureau of Public Procurement to carry out procurement audits.

questions.question: Which section stipulates the mandatory contents of standard bidding documents
questions.explanation: Section 25 requires procuring entities to use standard bidding documents containing clear specifications and criteria.

questions.question: Which section empowers the BPP to blacklist or debar contractors found guilty of misconduct
questions.explanation: Section 57 empowers the BPP to blacklist or debar contractors found guilty of misconduct.

questions.question: **Section 16** of the **PPA** requires bidders to demonstrate they have fulfilled obligations regarding:
options: Taxes, pensions, and social security contributions.
questions.explanation: Section 16 requires bidders to have fulfilled obligations regarding taxes, pensions, and social security contributions.

questions.question: Which method is allowed under the **PPA** when goods or services are available only from a limited number of suppliers
questions.explanation: Section 40 allows Restricted Tendering when goods or services are available only from a limited number of suppliers.

questions.question: Under section 42, when is direct procurement typically allowed?
questions.explanation: Section 42 allows direct procurement only in limited circumstances such as declared emergencies, not for routine convenience.

questions.question: Section 14 of the PPA prohibits procurement without what condition?
questions.explanation: Section 14 requires prior appropriation, meaning procurement should not proceed unless budget provision and funds are in place.

questions.question: Which section of the **PPA** permits negotiations only under limited circumstances, such as single-source procurement
questions.explanation: Section 36 permits negotiations only under limited circumstances, such as single-source procurement.

questions.question: Procedures for procuring consultant services, including selection and negotiation, are provided under which section of the **PPA**'
questions.explanation: Section 43 provides procedures for procuring consultant services, including selection and negotiation.

questions.question: Which section allows for the engagement of procurement consultants to assist procuring entities
questions.explanation: Section 45 provides for the engagement of procurement consultants to assist procuring entities.

questions.question: Which body must approve the release of funds for emergency procurements, subject to later audit and ratification
options: The Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Minister authorizes emergency procurements, often in line with procurement laws and thresholds, subject to later review Reference: Procurement – Emergency.

questions.question: Which section empowers the BPP to blacklist contractors for procurement misconduct
questions.explanation: Section 57 empowers the BPP to blacklist or debar contractors found guilty of misconduct.

To strictly enforce due workflow principles of transparency, competition, and efficiency.

Only to the internal audit unit. □.

questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which document sets out the responsibilities of an Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Financial Regulations book, Chapter.). Chapter 16 of the Financial Regulations book is dedicated to "Self Accounting Status" and outlines these responsibilities..
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: What does the Due Process policy seek to ensure to prevent extra-budgetary spending in MDAs?
options: Freedom for MDAs to spend outside approved budgets.
Funding of projects without budget provision.
Certification and funding only for duly appropriated projects.
questions.explanation: Due Process aims to stop extra-budgetary spending by ensuring that only projects with proper appropriation are certified and funded.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: responsibility for is the regulatory authority in charge for setting standards and developing the legal framework for public procurement in Nigeria belongs to which role?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Bureau of Public Procurement). The Bureau of Public Procurement is one of the regulatory authorities in charge for monitoring and oversight, and for setting standards and developing the legal framework for public procurement.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: What Due Process objective applies to project conceptualization?
options: Project conceptualization and packaging should match annual appropriation priorities and targets.
Projects should be disconnected from annual appropriations.
Conceptualization should be left to external consultants only.
Projects should be conceptualized on an ad hoc basis.
questions.explanation: Due Process requires project conceptualization and packaging to align with the priorities and targets already defined in the annual financial appropriations.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: What does the term Accounting Officer mean in official procurement practice?
options: The Permanent Secretary or head responsible for human, material, and financial resources.
An officer who keeps a cash book.
The Chief Executive of a parastatal in every case.
Any officer involved in financial transactions.
questions.explanation: In official practice, the Accounting Officer is the Permanent Secretary or equivalent head with responsibility for human, material, and financial resources.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: What is one duty of the Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry?
options: To conduct external audits of the ministry.
To ensure staff comply with the Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code.
To directly disburse all public funds.
questions.explanation: One duty of the Head of Finance and Accounts is to ensure that staff under that office comply with the Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: Which of the following officers is not listed as a Sub-Accounting Officer under Financial Regulation 115(ii)?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 115(ii) lists officers such as the Sub-Treasurer of the Federation, Federal Pay Officer, and Pensions Disbursement Officer as Sub-Accounting Officers. A Minister is the Political Head under Financial Regulation 116, not a Sub-Accounting Officer.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: What kind of checks should the Accountant-General or Accounting Officer use to supplement routine checks on Revenue Collectors' cash books?
options: Surprise inspections at the offices concerned.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 211 requires the Accountant-General or Accounting Officer to supplement routine checks on Revenue Collectors' cash books with surprise inspections at the offices concerned.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of necessitate for the abandonment of claims in the case of irrecoverable arrears of revenue?
options: The approval of the Accounting Officer.
The approval of the Head of Finance and Accounts.
The authority of the Minister of Finance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The authority of the Minister of Finance.). Financial Regulation 231 (i) states that the authority of the Minister of Finance is necessitate for any abandonment of claims, except where other authority is established by law.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: What is the limit of authority conveyed by recurrent expenditure warrants to officers controlling votes?
options: Unlimited authority at the Accounting Officer's discretion.
Authority limited to the approved amounts under each sub-head.
Authority limited to personal emoluments only.
Unlimited authority where cash remains available.
questions.explanation: Recurrent expenditure warrants authorize spending only up to the amounts approved under each relevant sub-head in the estimates.
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

questions.question: which role is primarily in charge for making disbursements of public funds?
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General and Accounting Officers.). Financial Regulation 401 (i) states: "All disbursements of public funds must be made by the Accountant General on the authority of the appropriate Warrant issued to him and also by Accounting Officers entrusted with the expenditure of public fund falling within the votes of charge of their ministries or extra ministerial offices and other arms, of government."
questions.chapter: Procurement Under Financial Controls - Expansion Set

subcategories.name: Audits, Sanctions & Compliance
subcategories.description: Audit controls, sanctions, and compliance obligations.

questions.question: The term **Audit Trail** refers to the sequential documentation of financial transactions to ensure:
options: Integrity and traceability, thereby ensuring accountability and transparency.
questions.explanation: Audit Trail refers to the sequential documentation of transactions to ensure integrity, traceability, accountability, and transparency Reference: Audits – Audit Trail.

questions.question: The type of internal control function that involves checking financial commitment before funds are paid, confirming legality and budgetary provision, is known as:
questions.explanation: Pre-audit functions involve checks before financial commitment is finalized to confirm legality and budgetary provision Reference: Audits – Types.

questions.question: The loss of government funds or property must be communicated immediately to:
options: The Head of the Civil Service and the Minister of Justice.
The Auditor-General of the Federation and the Accountant-General of the Federation.
The Minister of Finance and the National Assembly.
The officer's immediate supervisor and the Head of Accounts.
questions.explanation: Loss of government funds or property must be communicated immediately to the Auditor-General and the Accountant-General Reference: Audits – Loss Reporting.

questions.question: The maximum penalty for financial misconduct or fraud under the Financial Regulations may include:
questions.explanation: Public officers guilty of financial misconduct may face disciplinary action or prosecution, including surcharge and dismissal Reference: Sanctions – Penalties.

questions.question: The primary anti-corruption agency responsible for investigating and prosecuting financial crimes like fraud and money laundering is the:
questions.explanation: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) investigates and prosecutes financial crimes, fraud, and money laundering Reference: Sanctions – Anti-Corruption.

questions.question: Which body monitors compliance with anti-corruption laws, focuses on institutional corruption, and enforces ethical standards in public finance
questions.explanation: The Independent Corrupt Practices Commission (ICPC) focuses on institutional corruption and enforcement of ethical standards Reference: Sanctions – Anti-Corruption.

questions.question: Dismissal of an officer for serious financial misconduct must be supported by:
options: A direct memo from the Minister.
A Board of Inquiry report and internal investigation findings.
questions.explanation: Dismissal for financial misconduct must be supported by internal audit investigation findings and often requires a Board of Inquiry following due process Reference: Sanctions – Disciplinary Procedure.

questions.question: The concept of organizing financial tasks such that no single individual controls all stages of a transaction, thereby preventing fraud, is known as:
questions.explanation: Segregation of Duties is a crucial internal control mechanism designed to prevent fraud by distributing control over financial operations Reference: Audits – Internal Controls.

questions.question: Which concept describes mechanisms put in place to trace financial transactions and detect irregularities
questions.explanation: The Audit Trail documents the flow of transactions, and the Internal Audit monitors compliance to detect irregularities Reference: Audits – Control Mechanisms.

questions.question: The penalty for unauthorized expenditure, known as **Excess Vote**, is typically identified through:
options: The Auditor-General's Report on public accounts.
questions.explanation: The Auditor-General reports on all unauthorized or excess expenditure in their report on public accounts Reference: Audits – Reporting Excess.

questions.question: The use of government funds beyond the budget without subsequent legislative approval is classified as:
questions.explanation: Unauthorized expenditure is categorized as misappropriation or unauthorized spending Reference: Sanctions – Misappropriation.

questions.question: The final authority to restore a deferred increment, which requires assessing service improvement and conduct, lies with the:
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office holds the authority to decide on restoring a deferred increment (Rule 040208).

questions.question: If an officer causes financial loss, the penalty imposed (surcharge) is intended to recover:
options: The exact amount of financial loss caused by the officer's fault.
A fixed statutory amount unrelated to the loss.
questions.explanation: Surcharge is the recovery of government losses from the officer's pay, applied when the loss is due to the officer’s fault Reference: Sanctions – Surcharge Recovery.

questions.question: Which of the following bodies is empowered to grant special increments to an officer whose increment was previously withheld
options: The National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC) is the body empowered to grant special increments Reference: Sanctions – Special Increment Authority.

questions.question: The ultimate disciplinary penalty for serious misconduct, such as fraud or corruption, resulting in the forfeiture of all claims to retirement benefits, is:
questions.explanation: The ultimate penalty for serious misconduct is dismissal, resulting in the forfeiture of benefits, subject to the Pension Reform Act (Rule 100407) [142-144].

questions.question: Which officer's responsibility includes advising the Accounting Officer on the adequacy and functioning of the internal controls within the **MDA**'
questions.explanation: The Head of Internal Audit is responsible for independently evaluating financial activities and controls and advising the Accounting Officer Reference: Audits – Internal Auditor Role.

questions.question: The purpose of activating an **Audit Alarm** is to necessitate:
options: A detailed investigation into the suspected irregularity.
Automatic transfer of the implicated officer.
The suspension of all staff salary payments.
questions.explanation: An Audit Alarm necessitates a detailed investigation into the suspected irregularity flagged immediately Reference: Audits – Audit Alarm Action.

questions.question: In government audit working papers, what do accruals mean?
options: Cash is received before the related service is delivered.
Revenue or expense is recognized when it is incurred.
Budget reserves are kept aside by the Ministry of Finance.
Sanctions are imposed for delayed tax remittance.
questions.explanation: Accruals recognize revenue or expense when it is earned or incurred, not when cash is received or paid.

questions.question: Which type of audit, mandated under the **PPA**, involves the review of a bidder's documentation to confirm eligibility after the bid opening
questions.explanation: Post-Qualification audit is the review of bidder’s documentation to confirm eligibility after bid opening Reference: Audits – Procurement Audit.

questions.question: What is the penalty for an officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts
options: Required to resign or be withdrawn from the service.
Suspension until they pass the exam.
questions.explanation: An Officer who fails the confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts shall be required to resign or be withdrawn from the service (Rule 030104).

questions.question: Which agency is responsible for enforcing ethical standards in public office, particularly by receiving and verifying public officers’ asset declarations
options: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC).
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB) receives asset declarations and monitors compliance with ethical standards Reference: Sanctions – Ethics Enforcement.

questions.question: When issuing an Audit Query (**FR** 1204), the Accounting Officer must address the formal response to
questions.explanation: The Audit Query is a formal question from auditors, and the response must be addressed back to the originating authority (the issuing Auditor) for resolution Reference: Audits – Query Response.

questions.question: The Auditor-General's Report primarily focuses on the review of:
questions.explanation: The Audit Report focuses on the review of public accounts (past expenditure and revenue) and is critical for legislative oversight Reference: Audits – Legislative Review.

questions.question: To whom must an Officer promptly report a criminal charge or the outcome of such charge, under disciplinary action, whenever such event occurs
options: His Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: An Officer must promptly report a criminal charge or the outcome of such charge to his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office (Rule 100409).

questions.question: The disciplinary authority responsible for exercising control over Officers in the Federal Civil Service (**GL 07** and above) is vested in the:
options: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The power to dismiss and to exercise disciplinary control over Officers in the Federal Civil Service is vested in the Federal Civil Service Commission (Rule 100102).

questions.question: Which specific body has delegated full disciplinary powers to Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices in respect of officers on Salary **GL 13** and below
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The FCSC delegates disciplinary powers to Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices for officers on GL 13 and below [156-158] Reference: Sanctions – Delegated Powers.

questions.question: If the **FCSC** does not approve the Officer's dismissal or impose any penalty, the Officer shall be reinstated forthwith and be entitled to the full amount of salary denied him if he was interdicted or suspended, as stipulated by:
questions.explanation: If the Commission does not approve the Officer’s dismissal, he shall be reinstated forthwith and be entitled to the full amount of salary denied him if he was interdicted or suspended (Rule 100307(xi)).

questions.question: Which of the following acts constitutes **Gross Misconduct** under the Civil Service Handbook
questions.explanation: Fraud and falsification of records constitute gross misconduct in the civil service Reference: Sanctions – Gross Misconduct.

questions.question: The misconduct described as absenteeism without official permission is classified as:
questions.explanation: Absenteeism without permission is classified as gross misconduct Reference: Sanctions – Absenteeism.

questions.question: Which committee's recommendation forms the basis for the discipline (including removal from office) of officers on Salary **GL 06** and below by the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
questions.explanation: Discipline and removal of Officers on GL 06 and below is based on the recommendation of the Junior Staff Committee (JSC) (Rule 100104).

questions.question: Concealment of facts or any false statement made in an application for Government employment shall be regarded as sufficient grounds for:
options: Granting probation only, under the applicable service framework.
Non-engagement or subsequent termination of appointment without notice.
Reduction in rank, in line with formal public service practice.
Annual salary freeze, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Concealment of facts or any false statement in an application for employment shall be regarded as sufficient grounds for non-engagement or subsequent termination of appointment without notice (Rule 100431).

questions.question: Which type of assessment report is often used as evidence of **General Inefficiency** (**Rule 100201**)
options: Financial Audit Report, under the applicable service framework.
Poor performance appraisals, within established administrative procedures.
Ministerial Tenders Board Minutes, in line with formal public service practice.
Security Clearance Report, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: General inefficiency is directly linked to performance and competence, captured in performance appraisals (Chapter 5).

questions.question: Before documentary evidence can be used against an officer during an inquiry (**Rule 100307(vii**)), the officer must previously have been provided with a copy thereof or given:
questions.explanation: No documentary evidence shall be used against the Officer unless he has previously been supplied with a copy thereof or given open access thereto (Rule 100307(vii)).

questions.question: The ultimate penalty for serious misconduct, resulting in the forfeiture of all claims to retirement benefits, leave, or transport grant, is subject to the provisions of the:
questions.explanation: Dismissal results in forfeiture subject to the provisions of the Pension Reform Act, 2014 (as amended) (Rule 100407).

questions.question: The disciplinary process starts with a formal query issued by which officer
options: The Minister, under the applicable service framework.
The Auditor-General, in line with formal public service practice.
A Colleague, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: The disciplinary process starts with a query issued by the supervisor or Head of Department (Rule 100307(i)).

questions.question: An officer involved in procurement fraud, such as collusion or over-invoicing, faces which penalty under the Public Procurement Act (**PPA**) framework (**Section 58**)
options: Dismissal or criminal charges (imprisonment and fines).
questions.explanation: Section 58 prescribes imprisonment and fines for procurement-related fraud, which constitutes gross misconduct leading to dismissal.

questions.question: Which of the following disciplinary penalties is generally **prohibited** under the Financial Regulations (**FR** 3401)
questions.explanation: Disciplinary penalties may include surcharges, reductions, or warnings, but imposing monetary fines on officers as a penalty is generally prohibited Reference: Sanctions – Prohibited Penalties.

questions.question: The expression 'serious financial embarrassment' (**Rule 100414**) refers to an officer's state of indebtedness which has actually caused serious financial hardship to him and which may be prejudicial to:
options: The general meaning of the said expression.
The ability of the government to pay him.
questions.explanation: Serious financial embarrassment relates to indebtedness causing hardship prejudicial to the general meaning of the expression (Rule 100414).

questions.question: If an officer is suspected of **General Inefficiency**, the superior officer must first administer instruction and warning, and give the officer adequate time to redeem his image, for the objective of improving the officer's:
questions.explanation: The objective is to improve the officer's usefulness and efficiency (Rule 100201 practice).

questions.question: The minimum period for which an increment deferment must be fixed is:
questions.explanation: If an increment is deferred, the period must be fixed for not less than six months (Rule 040205).

questions.question: Which of the following disciplinary measures can be taken against a Permanent Secretary found guilty of misconduct (**Rule 100702**)
questions.explanation: Disciplinary measures against a Permanent Secretary include dismissal, termination, or retirement (Rule 100702).

questions.question: An officer, upon resumption from casual or special leave within the country, provided he spends such leave in Nigeria, is NOT required to have whom appointed to act in the duty post in question
questions.explanation: An acting Officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of his acting appointment by proceeding on casual or special leave within the country, and it is not necessary during any such leave to appoint another Officer to act in the duty post in question (Rule 020708(a)).

questions.question: The dismissal of Officers on **GL 06** and below requires the ultimate approval of which body, despite the involvement of the Junior Staff Committee (JSC)
questions.explanation: The dismissal of Officers on GL 06 and below is an exception to the delegated powers and still requires the approval of the FCSC (Rule 100103).

questions.question: If an officer resigns from the Service, they will forfeit all claims to vacation leave, and any leave privileges granted will be:
questions.explanation: An Officer who resigns will forfeit claims to vacation leave, and any leave privileges granted will be ex-gratia (Rule 020904(i)).
